last_excerpt: drop slides in, surface tension domes the cap. No more can be taken without spill, and yet nothing in the pattern says "finished" except the bulge, the sense. --- What if the capacity is not known in advance? The process continues until resistance, overflow, denial. Like typing into a form that suddenly rejects more characters—character 256 is simply refused. You notice the ceiling by failing to exceed it. --- Discrete tokens, finite slots. Not a continuous field, but a structure of yes/no, present/absent, filled/vacant. Each step toward completion is an irreversible act; you cannot un-write the ink once it dries, cannot un-mark the square once checked. --- A puzzle with all pieces in place: the absence of vacancy, every shape snugged against its neighbor, nothing left to do. There is no *signal* in the completion, only the silence of done. --- Some endings are not events, but constraints made visible.
last_concepts: the last ice cube melting in a glass
